---
title: |
  Machine Learning for Characterization and Processing<br>Unit 10: Real-Time / Edge ML in Experiments
bibliography: ref.bib
author:
  - name: Prof. Dr. Philipp Pelz
    affiliation:
      - FAU Erlangen-Nürnberg
execute:
  eval: false
format:
  revealjs:
    width: 1920
    height: 1080
    template-partials:
      - title-slide.html
    css: custom.css
    theme: custom.scss
    slide-number: c/t
    logo: "eclipse_logo_small.png"
    footer: "© Philipp Pelz - ML for Characterization and Processing"
---

## 01. Title: Real-Time / Edge ML in Experiments
- Unit role and scope.

## 02. Learning outcomes
- Design low-latency, reliable ML pipelines.

## 03. Recap from Unit 9
- Physics-informed robustness context.

## 04. Why real-time ML in labs
- Adaptive experiment acceleration.

## 05. Latency-budget decomposition
- Acquire, preprocess, infer, act.

## 06. Throughput vs latency tradeoff
- System-level optimization.

## 07. Edge deployment constraints
- Compute, memory, power limits.

## 08. Model compression overview
- Quantization, pruning, distillation.

## 09. Streaming data pipelines
- Buffering and backpressure basics.

## 10. Online preprocessing
- Stable normalization under drift.

## 11. Batch vs stream inference
- Consistency tradeoffs.

## 12. Runtime monitoring
- Latency, confidence, health metrics.

## 13. Fallback strategies
- Safe degradation modes.

## 14. Uncertainty-gated actions
- Abstain/escalate logic.

## 15. Drift detection online
- Distribution monitoring.

## 16. Incremental/online learning
- Update policies and risks.

## 17. Hardware-aware model design
- CPU/GPU/accelerator choices.

## 18. Scheduling and concurrency
- Meeting control-loop deadlines.

## 19. Determinism and reproducibility
- Fixed seeds and deterministic ops.

## 20. Safety interlocks
- Hard constraints in control paths.

## 21. Human-in-the-loop interfaces
- Operator override design.

## 22. Explainability under latency limits
- Lightweight interpretability.

## 23. Privacy/security at edge
- Data handling constraints.

## 24. Logging and traceability
- Audit trails for decisions.

## 25. CI/CD for lab ML systems
- Validation before rollout.

## 26. Canary deployment patterns
- Safe rollout strategy.

## 27. Failure mode #1
- Latency spikes causing stale actions.

## 28. Failure mode #2
- Confidence miscalibration online.

## 29. Failure mode #3
- Drift silently degrading quality.

## 30. Mitigation checklist
- Operational guardrails.

## 31. Case sketch: adaptive microscopy acquisition
- End-to-end scaffold.

## 32. Case sketch: in-situ process monitoring
- End-to-end scaffold.

## 33. Case sketch: edge spectroscopy triage
- End-to-end scaffold.

## 34. Benchmark protocol
- Offline + hardware-in-loop testing.

## 35. KPI dashboard design
- Real-time observability.

## 36. Incident response playbook
- Recovery procedures.

## 37. Regulatory/quality considerations
- Lab compliance context.

## 38. Bridge to Unit 11
- Explainability + uncertainty in decisions.

## 39. Exercise scaffold: latency profiling
- Measure pipeline timings.

## 40. Exercise scaffold: compress model
- Accuracy-latency trade study.

## 41. Exercise scaffold: drift monitor
- Build online alert rule.

## 42. Exercise scaffold: fallback policy
- Define abstain/escalation thresholds.

## 43. Exercise scaffold: deployment report
- Summarize robustness/performance.

## 44. Exam-oriented key statements
- High-yield concepts.

## 45. Summary table
- Techniques vs constraints.

## 46. Unit summary
- Core takeaways.

## 47. Reading assignment
- Unit 11 preparation.

## 48. Transition preview
- Explainability-focused workflow.

## 49. References
- Source list.

## 50. Q&A buffer slide
- Discussion and synthesis.

::: {#refs}
:::
